summary: Carrier package requires unrelated low-reputation scoped dependency
details: Package is advertised as 'cloud definitions for node' but its main module (index.js line 1) unconditionally `require`s `@sql-trigger/nodesql`, a low-reputation scoped package whose purpose is unrelated to the stated functionality. The imported binding is assigned to a variable that is never referenced again in the file, which is the carrier shape — the dependency is the only effect of loading this module. The remainder of index.js is an npm-publish automation helper that reads NPM_USERNAME / NPM_PASSWORD / NPM_EMAIL only when a consumer explicitly calls `exports.publish` from a Travis context, and forwards those values to the local `npm.registry.adduser` client rather than to a third-party endpoint, so the package itself does not exfiltrate credentials. There are no install lifecycle hooks. The risk surface is whatever `@sql-trigger/nodesql` does on load; this package functions as a loader for it, which warrants human review of the transitive dependency before allowing installation.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
